message:
Fix silent script failure in Gemini EBADF patch hook cleanup check mode

The Node.js one-liner that prints dead hooks could fail with a non-zero
exit code (e.g. when JSON.parse encounters edge-case input or Node
itself exits non-zero), which under set -e would abort the entire
script during --check mode. This is purely a diagnostic print — its
failure should never halt the script.

Added "|| true" guard to the dead-hooks display command on line 887 so
that check-mode hook enumeration is resilient to transient Node errors
while still printing the list when it succeeds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -884,7 +884,7 @@ else
                 fi
             elif [[ "$MODE" == "check" ]]; then
                 printf "  ${WRENCH}  ${YELLOW}settings.json — %d dead hook(s) found${RESET}\n" "$DEAD_COUNT"
-                "$NODE_BIN" -e "JSON.parse(process.argv[1]).forEach(h => console.log('     ' + h))" "$DEAD_HOOKS" 2>/dev/null
+                "$NODE_BIN" -e "JSON.parse(process.argv[1]).forEach(h => console.log('     ' + h))" "$DEAD_HOOKS" 2>/dev/null || true
                 inc_failed
             else
                 detail "Found $DEAD_COUNT dead hook(s), removing..."
